profile.work_background:
I advice CEOs, founders, and leadership teams through strategic advising, curated peer Circles, and Council-style sessions. With a background in brand strategy and decades of global experience, I help leaders align who they are with how they lead—so their impact flows from clarity, not pressure. I work where business strategy meets human depth, unlocking coherence and meaningful change.

Feeling the pull to lead differently?   Wondering how to move forward with clarity, integrity, and deeper impact?

→ From seasoned CEOs navigating complex change to visionary founders building from the inside out—I help leaders and their teams align who they are with how they lead.

I’m Vesna Lucca—Conscious Business Strategist, Advisor and creator of Corporate Unplugged –– an acclaimed leadership podcast and invite-only forum for people shaping the future. I connect people and insights that unlock clarity and lead to meaningful change. I help leaders align who they are with how they lead—so their impact flows from clarity, not pressure. 

With a background in brand strategy and decades of global experience, I support CEOs, founders, and their teams through strategic advising, curated peer Circles, and Council-style sessions that bring in diverse wisdom for complex decisions. I’ve helped leaders navigate brand reinventions, multi-country alignment, purpose recalibration, and cultural integration—always beginning from the inside out.

Whether guiding individuals or groups, I work at the intersection of business strategy and human depth—curating spaces for real conversation, clarity,  and transformative insight.

www.vesnalucca.com
www.corporateunplugged.com

Conscious Business Strategist. Founder. Advisor

Corporate Unplugged

Jan 2016 - Present · 10 yrs 5 mos

Greater Stockholm Metropolitan Area

I guide leaders on how to create companies that people love and respect. I call it the Soulprint method – a compass which guides people in a company to take the right actions daily and to stay the course, in any weather.

Thumbnail for Corporate Unplugged
Corporate Unplugged

The podcast puts the light on impact makers in business. In an informal and seriously playful way, Vesna Lucca is talking to people transforming business – entrepreneurs, leaders, activists, and other heroes from the business world, celebrities as well as hidden gems.

It is a stripped-down dialogue with people from all over the world who share their dreams, experiences and what they would never give up.

 Executive Coaching, Corporate Branding and +4 skills

Corporate Unplugged Forum 

Nov 2020 - Present · 5 yrs 7 mos

https://corporateunplugged.com/corporate-unplugged-forum/

Corporate Unplugged Forum is a space for leaders to explore ideas, learn, share, and design actions for a wiser future.

How can we make business a powerful force for good that truly cares about and listens to the lives it touches? Through expanded awareness and the dance between learnings and practice, positive change emerges and spreads. Through enquiry guided by questions rather than answers, we share what we learn as we apply it. We can create a good future for all life with the courage to care and act, creating human as well as economic value.

We believe that regenerative cultures and innovation can facilitate and accelerate personal development and the evolution of consciousness.

work.raw:
Left the corporate world in late 2015 to explore questions she wanted to dig into with people outside her existing business network. Describes herself as 'the fixer' — naturally drawn to helping companies and leaders become better through close collaboration. Works with companies through tailored experiences: small trusted peer groups facilitated monthly, curated 'pop-up brain trust' councils matched to specific company needs, leadership forums, team and culture workshops, speaking and dialogue hosting. [Guide: Your line 5 is about pure practicality — you're here to change the way people behave. You are the natural editor, the curator. When something isn't working, your conscience speaks with a voice that says 'this doesn't stick.'] Works primarily with people rather than companies — the format is secondary. Resists boxing things into fixed models or methodologies; prefers a dynamic, organic approach. Uses different tools from her 'toolbox' depending on the situation. Has 'endless curiosity' — always asking questions to understand, not to control. Currently working with an Italian company portfolio of 22 CEOs, exploring how to highlight and strengthen the important parts of their existing culture. Occasionally collaborates with other facilitators — values moving in and out of group work rather than always working alone.

--- From transcript: VESNA 2.txt ---
Facilitation and guiding transformative experiences—bringing people into spaces where they can grasp what is important. Describes herself as creating 'practical opportunities for unity.' Drawn to working with adults in companies and younger people on journeys. Notices what is missing in conversations and dynamics—'there is music here, but there is something missing'—and zooms in on that absence. Uses a black wall backdrop in her podcast, no cv, no art—'just you.' Her presence and way of communicating opens people up. Reads the room intensely rather than listening to details, feeling responsibility to 'get it right' because the moment with participants is unique and consequential. [Guide: Notes her line 5 'the Fixer'—power and projection. When she speaks genuinely from the heart, new worlds open. Her greatest challenge revolves around the impact of her voice and hidden projection or power dynamics it may elicit from others.]

--- From transcript: VESNA 3.txt ---
Vesna describes herself as operating naturally as a 'fixer' — scanning environments, synthesizing information that only she can take, then giving it back to the group. She has an intuitive curatorial sense: deciding what deserves attention and 'airtime', what can change the world, and what isn't ready. She commits deeply to projects she feels meet her inner standards, yet can easily park or even discard work that doesn't resonate, no matter how much effort went into it. She is exploring creating a 'living body of information' from her top podcast voices — something that evolves, is added to, changed over time, rather than a static archive. The concept of a book as both structure and portal appeals to her. She mines for wisdom in her Corporate Unplugged work, seeking to keep 'magic alive' within structures without stifling it.

--- From transcript: VESNA 4.txt ---
Vesna describes her work as holding space for collective learning and evolution through the Unplug forum — creating a 'space holder' where people can learn from each other without prescriptive outcomes. She mentions work with family-owned companies where she helps them 'free the soul of the company' rather than just liberating employees. She speaks of helping leaders remember what was there before, potentially redesigning what exists. There's a tension she identifies: people who become more liberated and aware in organizations often leave, creating a trap in her work of 'freeing slaves.' She questions whether corporate work enslaves her to 'someone else's thinking' (referencing the 56th shadow). The work seems to involve transforming organizations from 'self to we,' helping individuals find what's inside them rather than building around the organization as an organism. Guide note: Her Gene Key shows enrichment as a gift — she enriches others by sharing what she has learned in life, serving as a role model through lived experience.

--- From transcript: VESNA 5.pdf ---
Vesna works as a Conscious Business Strategist and Advisor. She describes herself as "a connector of people and insights that unlock clarity and lead to meaningful change." She helps leaders align who they are with how they lead. Her background spans over two decades in global corporate leadership roles, navigating complexity and leading brand and strategy engagements across industries and cultures. She has worked with entrepreneurs, creatives, and executives, helping them "shape brands, navigate change, and bring deeper meaning to their work." She offers: 1:1 strategic advising for leaders seeking clarity and catalytic movement; curated peer groups called Unplugged Circles; The Council—a handpicked constellation of wisdom convened for complex decisions; leadership forums and facilitated sessions; team and culture workshops focused on values and brand alignment; and speaking and dialogue hosting. She draws on a handpicked network of outstanding collaborators when needed to co-create bespoke solutions. Her work includes brand reinventions, multi-country alignment, purpose recalibration, and cultural integration—always beginning "from the inside out." Examples of outcomes: guided a CEO through identity shift and reinvention; created clarity and trust across multi-country leadership teams; co-created strategic brand platforms aligned with deeper values; supported brand evolution toward regenerative, future-facing business models; helped founders reconnect with their deeper voice. She describes her approach as creative by nature: "I connect dots, ideas, and people in unexpected ways—not for novelty, but for meaning." Rather than lead with fixed steps, she attunes to what's present: "I listen closely. I read the room, follow the energy, and co-create with those in it—so the process becomes a living dialogue, not a prewritten script." She founded the Corporate Unplugged Forum in 2020—an invite-only space for global leaders to explore ideas, learn, share, and design actions for a wiser future. She also hosts the acclaimed Corporate Unplugged podcast, recognized in the top 5% for content quality and production excellence, featuring guests like Simon Sinek, Seth Godin, Amy Edmondson, and others. In 2024, she co-authored *Rays of Truth* alongside 25 peers from the Forum—a collective book answering "What do I know to be true now—and how did I come to that realisation?" She describes this as "a shared pause," not a how-to.

qa_answers.0: I create the space where truth surfaces—and help leaders live it, without force.
qa_answers.1: Holding the mirror where people remember who they are—and move from there.
qa_answers.2: Create spaces where life remembers itself through people.
qa_answers.3: By expecting outcomes, I tighten my grip—when my gift lives in letting go and trusting what wants to emerge.
qa_answers.4: Between habit and awareness—but increasingly choosing to trust, soften, and let life lead.

lens.raw:
Experienced two to three months lying horizontal after a fall, which became 'a way of putting me horizontal, saying just stay there now.' That period gave permission to do less and be more — became an observer, more reflective, took time to read and think. 'I was reading up on things. I was more reflective and I was more taking the time for that. More like observing things happening around me, who was around me, what was going on. So it was a little bit of an observer mode.' Learning to navigate between hanging on too tight and being too flaky — between over-commitment and under-commitment. [Guide: You're working with the shadow of expectation, the gift of detachment, and the siddhi of celebration. You live in seven-year cycles — things have to begin and end. Your work is about coming to terms with the cycle of life and death. This struggle with the deep questioning about life and death, what is the purpose of life, was this a life well lived — these things are at the core of your Gene Key.] Deeply shaped by the transition out of corporate in 2015 — felt free, like she could breathe again, but also lonely after being used to a lot of people and resources. Spent five to six months in 'some kind of a vacuum bubble' where she could design something on her own. Has been integrating wisdom from many 'capable voices, people with good hearted and sharp mind' over the past decade through the podcast. Mother once came out of a coma saying 'I didn't leave because you and your brother are not ready yet' — grasping and not wanting to let go. Still learning what life would be like without her mother. Now in a stage of discovering what she's hanging on to that may already be complete.

--- From transcript: VESNA 2.txt ---
Life has been teaching her about relationships as 'the sand'—what everything else is built on. Currently learning what it means to succeed and how to redefine success—this is a persistent question she cannot shake. Moved from Apple and corporate roles into creating Corporate Unplugged and a podcast series. Previously focused on 'daily black and white stuff' but now stepping into questions of meaning and legacy. Learning the dance between panic, pause, and passion. [Guide: Gene Key 32—shadow of failure, gift of preservation, siddhi of veneration. Her field of evolution deals with relationships and what she is here to learn revolves around legacy and the great chain of being.] Reflecting on ancestry—recently told by two elderly Serbian relatives (both 80+) that her essence resembles her grandfather, who died when she was 4. He lost five children, his wife when young, went through war, saw his son killed in front of him. She has 'luxury problems' by comparison but carries his imprint. Serbian but not living there—questions ancestry, culture, and what transcends borders.

--- From transcript: VESNA 3.txt ---
Vesna has moved through episodes of loneliness, which she now observes 'from above' rather than being consumed by them. She recognizes loneliness is not 'for real' — it shifts depending on environment and a sense of belonging. She has become much less judgmental, both toward others and herself, replacing frustration with curiosity. She is learning to pause, give space, and see what unfolds next. She describes becoming 'more forgiving, patient' — especially with herself — and observing rather than correcting. She is refining her ability to 'collect the pieces that are most alive' and create something more whole, without fleeing or judging past environments. A recurring visual: herself old, alone, sitting on a bench with only two small leather bags — 'a clean slate', having gone through things, leaving nothing for others to clean up after her.

--- From transcript: VESNA 4.txt ---
Vesna has spent her life moving through trial and error to positions she now sees reflected in the Gene Keys structure. She describes a three-month period of physical immobility after falling down steps — a forced 'ultra deep dive into introspection' that became a time for redesigning and 'taking the next step in expressing my true self.' During this period, she experienced constant pain that couldn't be managed with medication, learning to accept rather than fight it. She speaks of this as 'paying respect to who I am and how I serve.' The experience felt 'refreshing, exciting, and true' — less from ego, more from an observer point of view, 'with a relaxed muscle.' She identifies a pattern of always questioning: 'Could I have done better? Could I have done more?' — a constant question mark that may be habitual conditioning rather than true intuition. She mentions moving from a cultural context (Serbia) into Western work structures with their particular expectations about professionalism and productivity. Her learning includes understanding the difference between distraction and what genuinely enriches her.

--- From transcript: VESNA 5.pdf ---
Vesna's journey has taught her that "real transformation comes not from adding more, but from remembering what matters." She discovered that "beneath the strategy and speed, there is a quieter truth—the part of us that already knows." She states: "Clarity isn't something we chase. It's something we remember when we slow down enough to hear it." The Corporate Unplugged Forum was born from "a longing for deeper connection—where leaders could explore what truly matters, beyond roles or expectations, and evolve together in trust and truth." She positions her work as creating space "where leaders soften, and something real begins to move again. This is not about fixing. It's about fine-tuning." She frames her facilitation as "presence that ripples, and leadership that feels like coming home." She writes: "I do this work to create space for resonance to return." Her multi-cultural background and global network continue to inform her work. She is guided by three core principles: "Connection. Coherence. Clarity."
lens.theme: Learning when to let go of what is already complete, trusting that preservation means honoring wholeness rather than preventing endings.
lens.summary: Vesna lives at the threshold between holding on and letting go, learning what it means to honor completion rather than fear it as failure. Her genius emerges in the sacred pause — the observer who can witness life's natural cycles without grasping or collapsing. Through stillness and reflection, she is discovering that preservation isn't about clinging to what was, but venerating what is already whole.

insights.title: Finding my inner voice and rhythm through nature and pause
insights.body: Vesna's body once gave her permission to stop grasping — lying horizontal for months, forced into observer mode, learning that being still wasn't failure but a different kind of work. She spent years building platforms and gathering voices, always adding more, until recently she began asking what might already be complete. Now she's discovering that her genius isn't in endless accumulation or perfect preservation, but in recognizing when a cycle has served its purpose and can be honored by letting it close.

lens.web_heading: The Observer Who Learns to Let Go
lens.web_intro: I spent three months lying horizontal after a fall — my body forced me into a stillness I would never have chosen. It became a way of putting me horizontal, saying just stay there now. I read, I watched, I noticed who stayed close and who disappeared. I became an observer. That period taught me something I'm still learning: that preservation isn't about holding on forever. It's about recognizing when something has served its purpose and can be honored by letting it close. Life keeps teaching me the difference between grasping and tending, between being too flaky and hanging on too tight.
highlights: I've moved through loneliness not by solving it but by learning to see it from above — it shifts depending on belonging, not circumstance.
I'm becoming less judgmental, more curious — especially with myself. I give space now where I used to correct.
I carry a recurring image: myself old, alone on a bench with two small leather bags. A clean slate. Nothing left for others to clean up.
My mother came out of a coma once and said she didn't leave because we weren't ready yet. I'm still learning what it means to need something that badly.
I've learned that adding more isn't transformation. Real clarity comes when I slow down enough to hear what already knows.
lens.web_closing: I see now that my work isn't about preventing endings — it's about venerating what is already whole.

qa_answers.1: Perhaps the very thing I most fear losing — whether it's my mother, my way of being, or the structures I've  built — is also what keeps me from discovering what's already complete. There's something in my body's memory of lying horizontal, being forced still, that might speak to the difference between grasping at legacy and letting life venerate itself through me. What if the challenge isn't about succeeding or failing, but learning when to let go?
qa_answers.2: Perhaps the misunderstanding lived in thinking I had to keep building on sand that was already shifting — that letting go of what was complete meant failure rather than the natural end of a cycle. My body put me horizontal once, gave me permission to stop grasping, and now something similar wants to happen in how I  hold success, legacy, the podcast, maybe even my role as mother. There may be a quiet knowing waiting beneath the panic about what preservation actually means for me— not holding on, but honoring what's already whole.
qa_answers.3: Maybe that completion and failure are not opposites,  but part of the same turning
qa_answers.4: Perhaps the teaching isn't about overcoming failure or even redefining success, but about recognizing that my genius lives in the sacred pause itself, in the observer who can hold both the hanging on and the letting go without collapsing into either.

field.raw:
Energized by working with people on the same wavelength — wanted to explore with people she didn't already know but felt attracted to. Values being able to 'breathe anew again' — the sense of oxygen and freedom. Finds it 'fun to do something with others occasionally and go in and out of these situations.' Thrives when able to talk freely without the constraint of translating between languages — wants to express herself clearly without the work of being her own translator. Drawn to people who are open to sharing and not boxed into their suit and role — 'pretty close to who they are somehow.' The podcast episodes are 'so many pearls just lying there collecting dust' — feels called to give them attention and devotion again, to dig in and see what universal wisdom can be cherished and presented differently. Works best when she can flow between different tools and approaches rather than following a fixed model. Restored by reflection time — reading, observing, being horizontal. [Guide: Your field is about celebration as an internal thing, not flashy parties. It's a personal smile, a victory of saying 'I'm not scared of death anymore, I'll be okay. I feel lighter.' You need conditions where you can detach from outcomes and trust that life offers a full range of outcomes, all interesting and beautiful.]

--- From transcript: VESNA 2.txt ---
Restored by the ocean—when walking by the sea, 'thinking about nothing somehow.' The continuous movement of waves is peaceful, makes her present. 'Things are coming and going, but it's part of the same source, so you can just kind of rest in it.' This is where she experiences a sense of unity and home. The unplugged forum and circle of friends aligned with her work also provide this sense of wholeness—'you feel more whole when you are with' them. Energised by creating containers where people can experience moments of bliss, grace, and belonging. Does her best work when not panicking, when pausing and reading the room without forcing. [Guide: The waves lapping on rock for millions of years is an example of the Great Chain of Being—the unified field connecting all who have witnessed the same experience across time. This is practical unity.]

--- From transcript: VESNA 3.txt ---
Vesna thrives in environments where she can belong and feel part of a larger whole, yet she also requires solitude to build her inner light and spiritual clarity. She is a 'line one' on her spiritual angle — the hermit position — needing time alone to discover what life is about and to strengthen her aura. Her loneliness is not a shadow but a necessary condition for generating energy and fire within herself. She then brings that energy into social circles where she operates as a 'line five' — the fixer and curator for larger groups. The balance between solitude (where she sources from within) and service to others (where she synthesizes and distributes) is core to her vitality. She is drawn to the idea of creating structures that don't suffocate magic — environments that remain open, curious, and alive.

--- From transcript: VESNA 4.txt ---
Vesna says there's 'a part of me that can never rest' — not from ambition but from a sense of wanting to 'use the time in a meaningful way.' Her son once observed he'd never seen his mother lying on a sofa reading during a normal day. She rarely reads 'just for distraction' — it has to be 'relevant or interesting or intriguing or important enough.' She identifies with 'cold pressure inside that wants to transcend everything you create or build' — a constant curiosity and exploration driving her to see 'how can this be done?' There's something about 'letting things settle' that she's learning — the enrichment that comes from doing less with more, from giving things time (like a pie settling after baking). She speaks of the forum continuing because 'people are showing up' — the feedback loop of engagement as her primary KPI. She mentions struggling with boundaries: finding it 'difficult to not somehow find some way to help' when somebody asks. She references an example with a forum member (Olena) where she had to draw a line after years of passive non-engagement, feeling some guilt about being 'not forgiving' or 'not understanding enough.' The question of 'where is enough?' recurs for her.

--- From transcript: VESNA 5.pdf ---
Vesna thrives at "the intersection of business strategy and human depth—curating spaces for real conversation, clarity, and transformative insight." She holds space for "thoughtful leaders to reconnect with purpose and move forward with grounded confidence." She values intimate gatherings, retreats, and facilitated sessions designed for "shared insight, connection, and perspective." The Forum community is described as "leaders with good hearts and sharp intellects. Connected as compassionate human beings driven to creatively explore the complexity of our era." Testimonials reveal the conditions where her energy is alive: spaces that are "professional, intimate, and real" where "leaders get to be human again"; conversations that feel like "returning to what matters"; work characterized by "holding complexity without making it complicated." She describes her design aesthetic preference as "spacious and clean," "soft strength," with "generous white space" and "light, breathable layouts"—metaphors that likely reflect the energetic field she seeks to create. The mood she aims for is "unforced elegance," "soulful intelligence," "warm resonance, not performance." Visual metaphors she uses: "A circle of people speaking honestly in a quiet, sunlit room," "Light filtering through linen curtains—revealing just enough, never forcing attention."
field.summary: Vesna's radiance emerges in spaces where people show up without masks, aligned with their truth and unafraid to share it. She comes alive near water and in rhythm with nature, where the continuous motion of waves teaches her to trust what comes and goes. Her brightest work happens when she creates containers for bliss and grace, gathering people who put action behind their words and refuse to compromise their integrity.
field.web_intro: I am restored by the ocean — by walking near water and thinking about nothing, watching waves that have been moving for millions of years. That continuous motion makes me present. Things come and go, but they are all part of the same source, so I can rest in it. I need solitude to build my inner light, and I need circles of aligned people to bring that light into. My loneliness is not a shadow. It is the condition for generating the fire I then carry into rooms where real conversation can happen.
highlights: Time alone — reading, reflecting, being horizontal — to discover what life is about and strengthen what I carry
The ocean, and any place where I can be with nature's rhythm without forcing anything
Spaces where people show up without masks, aligned with their truth and unafraid to share it
Working with others on the same wavelength — people I am drawn to, not people I already know
Containers where I can pause, read the room, and create moments of bliss and grace without panicking
field.web_closing: I do my best work when I can flow between solitude and service, between building the fire and tending the circle.

qa_answers.0: I am happy with my environment, but need to stretch out into nature to truly find my voice. My environment becomes a mirror to my inner state, a place for them to merge - which keeps me looking dynamically for the places that best serve me. they are not fixed.
qa_answers.1: nature, being by the sea, feeling the rhythm of the waves lulling me into myself.
qa_answers.2: people who put their action where their mouth is. people with skill, integrity, those who do not give up on their values and mission no matter what.
qa_answers.3: people and places that are in alignment with themselves, thriving, expressing truth and beauty in their sheer being.
qa_answers.4: the importance of the rhythms of nature. it is not an abstract or outside need; but rather an inner calling to hear itself.

call.raw:
Wants to somehow have a positive impact on companies by working closely with leaders, 'make things happen, make it better, make it more serving.' But has realized 'it's not about the companies, it's about the people. The only thing that is enduring is if I can continually learn and grow myself as a soul, and if I can do that with others and help others.' Feels called to present work more as 'this is what I believe in' — her embodied philosophy integrated from experiences with many people and voices. Wants to be truthful and honest rather than packaging things in easy-to-buy marketing. Drawn to curating small groups and creating 'pop-up brain trust' councils for specific company needs. Feels tension between her natural resistance to fixed models and the need to articulate what she offers. Exploring how the podcast wisdom — nearly 170 episodes — might become 'a huge list of universal wisdom, just through the voices of these people,' possibly as a book or presented in different chunks across different spaces. [Guide: You're here to illuminate the crack where light comes in — to create wholeness. Your work is about detachment and celebration within the cycle of life and death. The question isn't what you do, but what you've let go of. Your highest work is changing the way people behave by helping them become aware — offering them permission to see there's possibility for change.]

--- From transcript: VESNA 2.txt ---
Persistent longing to create 'practical opportunities for unity'—bottling the experience of unity she feels by the seaside and bringing it into companies and circles where people can connect authentically. Wants to leave behind 'some kind of bridge'—a field where adults in companies or younger people on journeys can understand what is actually important, guided by 'important pillars of insight.' Her call involves redefining success and answering: What does it mean to live well? What is worth preserving for future generations? What is timeless? She is pondering legacy, rituals, containers—experiences that bring people into insights leading to wiser choices. [Guide: Her siddhi of veneration—ancestral reverence, the cycle of life and death, noble action. When she taps into this energy, she transforms and radiates in a way that attracts others. Pause. Listen. The heart and 'here' are connected. Her DNA can unravel and collect more light, leading her to become capable of more.] Feels this is not just about career but about leaving a mark that lasts beyond her.

--- From transcript: VESNA 3.txt ---
Vesna feels called to break through closed-circuit thinking and keep magic alive within structures — whether corporate, communal, or creative. She is drawn to the interplay between 'structure and magic', limitation and transcendence. The metaphor of the Japanese daisugi forestry technique resonates deeply: bringing newness from ancient roots without destroying the original tree. She wants to create something that serves beyond herself, that is a 'living vehicle' rather than a static product. Her purpose includes preparing for her own death with grace — leaving a clean slate, no excess baggage, having gone through things consciously. She is learning to observe the unfolding of natural laws (justice, balance) rather than impose judgment. There is a sense of deconstructing what has been built in order to return to essence, to radiate light, to grow her soul. [Guide: Richard Rudd suggests that when even one or two people manifest the siddhi of the 60th Gene Key — justice, divine law, transcendence of limitation — it has the power to reorganize history and society. Vesna's radiance (Gene Key 60) is about how light and matter come together, the merkabah, the balance between form and formlessness. Her work is to break the scaffolding of capitalism and limitation without losing the roots, to bring realism to idealism, to bottle magic within structure.]

--- From transcript: VESNA 4.txt ---
Vesna speaks of a 'wish' and 'call' during her injury period: 'using the time in a way of redesigning... take the next step in expressing my true self.' She describes this as 'paying respect to who I am and how I serve' — something that 'needed extra attention.' She's drawn to the question of how to 'transcend structure' — creating something and then finding what's beyond it. She identifies with 'celebrate life, drink from life fully' though initially uncertain if that was her purpose. The intoxication siddhi resonates: taking in the toxin and spitting out goodness as her 'ultimate challenge.' She mentions the forum as a space where 'when there is a special kind of magic in the air, ideas that nobody would have thought of might pop up' — if there's energy, they'll act on it. She's interested in connecting 'local heroes' to help their radiance spread in a more coherent way. There's an unresolved question about balancing meaningful work with not being enslaved to 'somebody else's thinking' — wanting to do work that genuinely improves people's lives in a 'sincere, honest way' that doesn't require a judge. Guide interpretation: Her purpose involves enriching others by sharing what she's learned, serving as a role model through lived experience. The thyroid connection suggests her purpose is tied to 'speaking her truth' and being in positions where people want to listen.

--- From transcript: VESNA 5.pdf ---
Vesna states: "I do this work to help [resonance] happen—quietly, clearly, and with care." Her purpose statement: "Because leadership rooted in clarity and presence creates lasting change. Because when leaders and their teams reconnect with what matters, they unlock more than results—they unlock resonance." She frames her calling as helping leaders move "beyond performance into clarity, coherence, and meaningful impact" and supporting those "ready for alignment, resonance, and clarity." She wants to help leaders "lead from the inside out" so their "impact flows from clarity, not pressure." The Forum represents her commitment to collective intelligence: "We build our collective intelligence by openly sharing our diverse perspectives and expertise. We have a deep desire to evolve, to find more ways to serve, and to deeply connect with people on the same quest." She describes her mission as creating spaces where leaders can explore "what truly matters" and "evolve together in trust and truth." Her newsletter is called *Resonance Notes*—"short reflections. Long resonance. Insight, not noise. Rooted in presence, written for those navigating complexity with integrity and care." This work is about "cre­ating space for resonance to return."
call.theme: I curate spaces where people pause long enough to remember what matters and find their own voice within something greater.
call.summary: Vesna creates practical opportunities for unity—curating circles, councils, and conversations where leaders and seekers can connect to what is timeless and true. Her work is about changing the way people behave by helping them become aware, offering permission to see there is possibility for change. She serves as gatekeeper and guide, bridging embodied wisdom with the questions that matter: What does it mean to live well, and what is worth preserving for future generations?
call.web_intro: I am here to create practical opportunities for unity — not as a concept, but as something people can touch and return to. The work is simple: curate spaces where people pause long enough to remember what matters, where leaders and seekers alike find their own voice within something greater. I want to leave behind a field — a living bridge — where the questions that shape a life can be asked honestly: What does it mean to live well? What is worth preserving? What is timeless? This is not career. This is about leaving a mark that serves beyond me.
highlights: Bottling the seaside unity I feel and bringing it into boardrooms and circles where people can connect authentically
Helping leaders move beyond performance into clarity, coherence, and the kind of impact that flows from the inside out
Building collective intelligence through openly shared perspectives — evolving together in trust and truth
Preparing for my own death with grace, leaving no excess baggage, having gone through things consciously
Breaking the scaffolding of what limits us without destroying the roots — structure and magic, held together
call.web_closing: When there is magic in the air, ideas nobody would have thought of might appear — if there is energy, we act.

qa_answers.2: ripples outward towards higher consciousness, living and working in alignment with our soul, with our essence. supporting people in whatever way I can so that their voices are heard.
qa_answers.3: I feel I am doing this.
qa_answers.4: I'm not sure. Perhaps the next breakthrough is in the listening, not the planning? What if the next side doesn't require me to resolve the tension between structure and magic, but to let that very tension become the living container where others can pause and unravel?
